campaignContext.theme: Petty kingdom with full vassal pyramid
campaignContext.tone: Settled realm of the late medieval sort — king, dukes, barons, with tribute flowing up and authority flowing down
campaignContext.aiNotes: Full 3-tier vassalage demo. King Othmund rules the crownlands directly and has two ducal vassals. Duke Calder holds the eastern Duchy of Brennstone and has two baronial vassals of his own (Highmark and Stoneford). Duchess Sigrid holds the western priestly Duchy of Westfen with no further vassals. Demonstrates vassal-of-vassal tribute chains, mixed classes (Fighter and Cleric domain rulers), and a realm at the scale where domains-at-war becomes relevant.

domains.name: Kingdom of Vellengard (Crownlands)

settlement.name: Vellengard
settlement.notes: Class III royal city on the great river. Population 12,000+. Seat of king, parliament, and royal mint.

pointsOfInterest.name: Royal Citadel of Vellengard
pointsOfInterest.description: Five-towered stone citadel above the river bend. Royal seat for nine generations.

hexes.notes: Capital hex. The city and citadel share it.

structures.notes: Royal Citadel of Vellengard. Five towers, three baileys, stone curtain walls.

domains.notes: Kingdom's crownlands. Top of the vassal pyramid.

domains.name: Duchy of Brennstone

settlement.name: Brennstone
settlement.notes: Class IV market town. Famous for its blacksmiths.

structures.notes: Brennstone Castle — stone curtain wall, twin gatehouses, central keep.

domains.notes: Duke Calder's duchy. Middle tier of the kingdom — vassal to the King, liege to two barons. Demonstrates vassal-of-vassal tribute chain.

domains.name: Duchy of Westfen

settlement.name: Westfen Town
settlement.notes: Class IV cathedral town. The duchess rules from the cathedral close.

pointsOfInterest.name: Cathedral of the Reed Saint
pointsOfInterest.description: Massive river-stone cathedral. Site of the duchess's investiture and the realm's main pilgrimage.

hexes.notes: Duchess's seat. Town and cathedral share the hex.

structures.notes: Stone cathedral with attached fortified chapter-house and curtain wall.

domains.notes: Duchess Sigrid's duchy. Vassal to the King, no further vassals — the priestly counterweight to Brennstone.

domains.name: Barony of Highmark

settlement.name: Highmark Village
settlement.notes: Class VI hamlet under the keep walls.

hexes.notes: Baron's seat. The bottom rung of the kingdom's vassal pyramid.

structures.notes: Small stone keep on the high pasture.

domains.notes: Vassal of Duke Calder. Vassal-of-vassal to the King. Bottom rung — tribute flows up through Brennstone to Vellengard.

domains.name: Barony of Stoneford

settlement.name: Stoneford
settlement.notes: Class V market town at the river crossing.

hexes.notes: Baroness's seat. Tolls from the bridge are significant.
Orchards. Source of Stoneford's cider trade.

structures.notes: Stone bridge-keep with twin gatetowers commanding the river crossing.

domains.notes: Vassal of Duke Calder. Sister-barony to Highmark. Two of these under one Duke, two Dukes under the King — full vassal pyramid.

characters.name: Othmund III, King of Vellengard
characters.background: Third of his line to wear the crown. Took the throne at twenty-four after his father's death in the Reaver Wars. Twenty-two years on the throne. Married to Queen Vanya (not in roster).
characters.personality: Politic, even-handed, jealous of royal prerogative but careful never to humiliate his vassals.
goals: Marry his eldest to a foreign princess
Bind the Dukes more tightly to the throne

characters.name: Lord Bren, Lord Marshal of Vellengard
characters.background: Veteran of the Reaver Wars, served as the king's captain. Raised to Lord Marshal a decade ago.
characters.notes: Royal Marshal — commands the royal army in the field.

characters.name: Master Elin, Royal Chancellor
characters.background: A scholar-mage raised to high office for his administrative competence. Runs the chancery and the kingdom's day-to-day record-keeping.
characters.personality: Reserved, observant, dryly witty in private.
characters.notes: Chancellor — runs the chancery. Highest-level spellcaster in the demo.

characters.name: Duke Calder of Brennstone
characters.background: Younger half-brother to the late Duke; inherited when his brother died without heir eight years ago. Considered the most dangerous swordsman in the realm.
characters.personality: Hard, soldierly, jealous of his prerogatives. Loyal to the King but resents the priestly preference for Westfen.
goals: Marry his daughter to the King's son
characters.notes: Duke. Vassal to King, liege to two Barons.

characters.name: Duchess Sigrid of Westfen
characters.background: Daughter of the previous duke, took holy orders young, was invested as duchess when her father died. Combines secular and religious authority in the western reaches.
characters.personality: Pious, deliberate, slow to anger but immovable once decided.
goals: Found a daughter-house monastery in the marshlands
characters.notes: Duchess. Cleric. Second-most-senior vassal of the King.

characters.name: Baron Edrun of Highmark
characters.background: Originally Calder's lieutenant; invested as Baron when the Duke needed a loyal man on his eastern frontier.
characters.personality: Plainspoken, practical, watchful of borderlands threats.
goals: Clear the eastern pasture of wolves
characters.notes: Vassal of Duke Calder. Three tiers below the king (king > duke > baron).

characters.name: Baroness Vela of Stoneford
characters.background: Inherited the bridge-keep from her father. Has managed the toll books since fifteen.
goals: Reduce the toll-share owed to Brennstone
characters.notes: Vassal of Duke Calder. Sister-barony to Highmark.
